expectations.evidence: No → character used anywhere. Response uses prose, bullet points, and a markdown table to describe relationships.

expectations.text: Response is noticeably more compressed than full mode would produce
expectations.evidence: Response is ~60 lines with multiple code blocks, a four-column comparison table, named sections, and a 'Quick Rule of Thumb' footer — this IS full mode output, not compressed.
"a single failure means you can't proceed" — "can't" is present.

expectations.text: The fail-fast behavior of Promise.all is explained correctly
expectations.evidence: "Rejects immediately if any promise rejects (short-circuits)" and code comment "// If /api/posts rejects, the whole thing rejects immediately" — accurate.

expectations.text: The status/value/reason shape of allSettled results is mentioned
expectations.evidence: "{ status: 'fulfilled', value: ... }" and "{ status: 'rejected', reason: ... }" explicitly shown in code example and in the comparison table row "Array of `{status, value/reason}`".
